content:
Given the undirected dynamic graph with the edges:
[(3, 8, 5), (4, 5, 5), (0, 6, 8), (0, 1, 10), (2, 7, 12), (0, 3, 13), (5, 6, 17), (2, 3, 18), (5, 7, 18), (1, 9, 20), (0, 7, 21), (0, 9, 22), (4, 6, 28)],

We need to find the nodes linked with node 0 exactly at time 13.

First, we will scan each edge (u, v, t) and collect the other node whenever the time t matches the queried time AND one of the nodes matches the queried node:

* Edge (0, 6, 8) has node 0 and time 8. -> Add node 6.
* Edge (0, 1, 10) has node 0 and time 10. -> Add node 1.
* Edge (2, 7, 12) has node 2 and time 12. -> Add node 7.
* Edge (0, 3, 13) has node 0 and time 13. -> Add node 3.
* Edge (5, 6, 17) has node 5 and time 17. -> Add node 6.
* Edge (2, 3, 18) has node 2 and time 18. -> Add node 3.
* Edge (5, 7, 18) has node 5 and time 18. -> Add node 7.
* Edge (1, 9, 20) has node 1 and time 20. -> Add node 9.
* Edge (0, 7, 21) has node 0 and time 21. -> Add node 7.
* Edge (0, 9, 22) has node 0 and time 22. -> Add node 9.
* Edge (4, 6, 28) has node 4 and time 28. -> Add node 6.

Now we have the following nodes matched at time 13: 3, 7, and 9.

Answer: [3, 7, 9]
